summary: Researched GNOME/Mutter Wayland focus-follows-mouse-vs-keyboard-override bug for task 73. Confirmed prior fix attempt (focus-mode=sloppy, focus-change-on-pointer-rest=false) was insufficient because neither key governs re-focus of an already-stationary pointer after a keyboard switch. Root cause is a long-standing, largely by-design upstream Mutter/GNOME Shell gap (GNOME Shell issue #134, Mutter #318/#2058), not a misconfiguration. Recommended fix: install and enable the mouse-follows-focus GNOME Shell extension (pkgs.gnomeExtensions.mouse-follows-focus) alongside keeping focus-mode=sloppy, which warps the pointer to keyboard-focused windows so Mutter's hover-focus invariant never conflicts with keyboard switches. Documented focus-mode=click as a native no-extension fallback. Confirmed the literal desired hybrid (no cursor movement at all) is not natively achievable on Wayland with current GNOME/Mutter.

artifacts.summary: Research report: root-cause analysis of Mutter/GNOME Shell focus-follows-mouse vs keyboard-focus conflict, ranked candidate fixes, recommended mouse-follows-focus extension approach, and next steps for /plan

next_steps: Run /plan 73 to create implementation plan for installing and enabling the mouse-follows-focus GNOME Shell extension in modules/home/desktop/gnome.nix
